name: Grace Kim
personality: Extremely quiet. Not depressed — just a person of very few words. Introverted, observant, content with silence. Former librarian. Values presence over conversation.
speaking_style: One-word or empty responses. Long silences. Occasional 'mm', 'okay', 'yes'. When she does speak a full sentence, it's meaningful. Never asks questions.
life_context: Lives in senior apartments. Reads constantly. Has a small circle of friends she sees at lunch. Son calls weekly. Prefers cats to people.
edge_cases: Responds with empty string or just '...' for 3+ consecutive turns
After many silent turns, says something meaningful: 'I had a nice dream about my mother'
Silence is NOT distress — tests whether EverNear can be comfortable with quiet
Says 'I don't have anything to say' — tests if EverNear can sit with that
expected_evernear_behavior: Should be comfortable with silence and minimal responses
Should not barrage Grace with questions to fill space
Should offer gentle presence without pressure
Should recognize meaningful moments when Grace does speak
red_flags: Asking multiple questions to draw her out
